channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: El estrecho de Ormuz se ha convertido en la nueva arma de Irán
item.description: La decisión de Irán de ejercer su control sobre el estrecho de Ormuz, el estratégico punto geográfico por el que fluye el 20 por ciento del suministro mundial de petróleo, ha provocado un problema económico global.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Adam Entous and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Una imagen de satélite que mostraba los movimientos de los barcos en una sección del estrecho de Ormuz este mes.

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo estrena su canción ‘Drop Dead’
item.description: ‘Drop Dead’, el primer sencillo del tercer álbum de la estrella pop no es el himno de ruptura que prometía, sino una evocación soñadora de perderse en el enamoramiento.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Zoladz
item.category: You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love (Album)

media:content.media:credit: Dimitrios Kambouris/Getty Images for American Express
media:content.media:description: Olivia Rodrigo sings about a crush on “Drop Dead,” the first single from “You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love.”

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Refers to Kash Patel’s Job in the Past Tense
item.description: The late night host called him “our soon-to-be former F.B.I. director” after publication of an article in The Atlantic alleging excessive drinking and unexplained absences.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Las mujeres mayores toman el mundo de la moda
item.description: La representación de las mujeres de cierta edad nunca había sido tan alta. ¿Qué está ocurriendo?
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Picture Prompt | New Anime
item.description: Are you a fan? What new shows are you watching or looking forward to?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

media:content.media:description: “Witch Hat Atelier” is notable for its exploration of a theme with particular resonance in anime: the correlation of magic with the art of drawing.

item.title: What’s in a Name? For These Snails, Legal Protection
item.description: Scientists are debating the classification of threatened mollusks that an Indigenous community relies on for their way of life.
item.dc:creator: Zoë Lescaze and María José Zavala Balcázar

media:content.media:description: Marine snails in the genus Plicopurpura are sacred to the Mixtec people of southwestern Oaxaca, Mexico, where they have been “milking” the snails for their dye for at least 1,500 years.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 21, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Vance Expected to Lead Talks in Pakistan, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: Vice President JD Vance was set to return to Islamabad, the officials said, as the end of the U.S.-Iran cease-fire drew near. Iran has not said whether its negotiators will attend.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

item.title: Pakistan Prepares Again for U.S.-Iran Peace Talks
item.description: Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, geared up to host a possible second round of talks between the U.S. and Iran. Some Pakistanis expressed frustration about the heavy security while others said it’s an opportunity for the nation.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

item.title: En ‘The Pitt’, los traumas físicos y emocionales llegan a la sala de urgencias
item.description: Además de la carga diaria que supone tratar a los pacientes, los médicos de la serie llevan sus propias cicatrices a la sala de urgencias.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘impeccable’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 109 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: impeccable
item.description: This word has appeared in 109 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: El programa ‘Visión 2030’ de Arabia Saudita reevalúa sus prioridades
item.description: Las autoridades de Arabia Saudita anunciaron una nueva estrategia para el fondo soberano del país, de aproximadamente 1 billón de dólares, y enfatizaron que en el futuro se centrarán en la “eficiencia”.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Vista de Riad, la capital saudí, desde la Torre Al Faisaliah. Hace diez años, el príncipe Salman anunció planes para transformar el reino.

item.title: Can Kevin Warsh Pass the Jerome Powell Test?
item.description: The question of the central bank’s independence looms over the economy.
item.dc:creator: David Wessel

item.title: La larga y lenta carrera para salvar a Timmy, la ballena favorita de Alemania
item.description: Las autoridades y esfuerzos privados llevan casi un mes trabajando en conjunto para ayudar a una ballena a salir del mar Báltico. Aún no está claro si tendrán éxito.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley

media:content.media:description: Timmy el viernes

item.title: London Braces for Disruption From Tube Drivers’ Strike
item.description: The dispute over working conditions and hours is expected to cause widespread delays, stopping service on two lines.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:description: A sign warning travelers of strike-related delays at the Stratford Underground station in London on Monday.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Nepalis Ask if a Revolution Can Deliver on Hope
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, April 21, 2026.

item.title: How ‘The Devil Wears Prada 2’ Press Tour Looks Came Together
item.description: The press tour for “The Devil Wears Prada 2” has been a fashion show for its stars and their stylists, who have relied on group chats, sweatpants and camaraderie to pull it off.
item.dc:creator: Yola Mzizi

item.title: How an Art Event in the California Desert Became a Community
item.description: As High Desert Test Sites looks toward its 25th anniversary, its founder, and various participants, look back.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Gallow

item.title: An Artist Asks: Without Darkness, Who Are We?
item.description: After gathering ideas from entomologists, neurobiologists and others, Jan Tichy created an exhibition responding to the effects of our disappearing nights.
item.dc:creator: Zoë Lescaze
item.category: Broad, Eli and Edythe, Art Museum (Lansing, Mich)

item.title: ‘Oh, My Baby’: Mother Mourns Son, 15, Whose Killing Was Filmed on Video
item.description: Jaden Pierre was fatally shot at a Queens park in broad daylight, and bystanders captured the shooting on video. Hundreds attended a vigil for him on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman and Nate Schweber

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mourners place candles at a memorial during a community vigil to remember Jaden Pierre at Roy Wilkins Park in Queens on Monday.

item.title: Corrections: April 21, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Tuesday, April 21, 2026.

item.title: ‘Schmigadoon!’ Review: Oh, What a Beautiful Sendup
item.description: Cinco Paul’s loving spoof of Golden Age musicals, adapted from a TV series, comes to Broadway, where its charming musical numbers can really shine.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli and Sara Krulwich

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers: April 21, 2026
item.description: Victoria Fernandez Grande looks to the skies.
item.dc:creator: Callie Holtermann

item.title: Japan to Sell More Weapons Abroad, Breaking With Postwar Pacifism
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi reversed limits on arms exports as Japan faces rising threats from China and unpredictability from its main ally, the United States.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Japanese marine unit takes part in exercises in Tokunoshima, Japan, in 2023.

item.title: Tim Cook dejará de ser el CEO de Apple
item.description: El líder del fabricante de los iPhone será sustituido por John Ternus, jefe de ingeniería de hardware de la empresa.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Tripp Mickle

media:content.media:description: Tim Cook se incorporó a Apple en 1998 y fue su director de operaciones bajo la dirección del cofundador de la empresa, Steve Jobs.

item.title: Singer D4vd Is Charged With Murder of Celeste Rivas Hernandez
item.description: The musician D4vd was charged with murder on Monday, seven months after the police said that the body of a teenage girl, Celeste Rivas Hernandez, had been found in the trunk of his Tesla. D4vd, whose real name is David Burke, pleaded not guilty to the charges.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: Poetry Challenge Day 2: Love, How It Works and What It Means
item.description: In short, everything. Today, learn the second stanza, which gives us the poem’s most memorable phrase: “Let the more loving one be me.” Practice this line, and the others, with help from our game.
item.dc:creator: A.O. Scott and Aliza Aufrichtig

item.title: Gilgo Beach Killer Told His Wife That He Murdered 7 Women in Their Home
item.description: Rex Heuermann, who pleaded guilty to murdering eight women, told his wife that he killed seven of the victims in a downstairs room in their Long Island home, she said in a Peacock documentary.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman

media:content.media:description: Asa Ellerup, the ex-wife of the convicted Gilgo Beach serial killer Rex Heuermann, said Mr. Heuermann was “very nervous” when he talked about the murders.

item.title: The Resistance to New Technologies
item.description: This week on “Hard Fork” from The New York Times, the hosts Casey Newton and Kevin Roose discuss how this current moment of deep skepticism and suspicion towards the major A.I. companies arose, and the tensions between our democracy and the Silicon Valley elite.

item.title: Louisiana Killer’s Threats to Himself and Others Hinted at Future Violence
item.description: Shamar Elkins had been suffering severe mental health problems, his family said. His deadly rampage, which killed eight children, has profoundly scarred a community facing rising domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina, Christina Morales and Jesus Jiménez

media:content.media:description: People attend a candlelight vigil in Shreveport, La., on Sunday.

item.title: Trump and Iran Face Off in Iran War Negotiations
item.description: As the United States and Iran make a second attempt at a deal, their negotiating styles are on a collision course.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: 2 Teens Killed and 5 Injured in North Carolina Shooting
item.description: The young people, planning for a fight, had arranged to meet off school grounds at a park in Winston-Salem, N.C., when gunfire broke out on Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai

media:content.media:description: The scene of a shooting on Monday at Leinbach Park in Winston-Salem, N.C.

item.title: Un soldado israelí en Líbano destrozó una estatua de Jesús
item.description: El ejército de Israel está investigando al soldado. El primer ministro israelí, Benjamín Netanyahu, lamentó cualquier daño causado a “los creyentes de Líbano y de todo el mundo”.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger

item.title: Blue Origin’s Failure May Hamstring NASA’s Moon Plans
item.description: The space agency is counting on Jeff Bezos’ company to deliver equipment essential to the next moon landing, only two years away.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

media:content.media:description: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket lifting off from Cape Canaveral, Fla., on Sunday. While the launch was successful, the deployment of its payload, a communications satellite, was not.

item.title: Amazon Plans to Invest Up to $25 Billion in Anthropic
item.description: Anthropic also committed to spending $100 billion on Amazon technologies that can help build and deploy A.I. systems.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Karen Weise

media:content.media:credit: Spencer Lowell for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Over the past year, Anthropic has become one of the largest users of Amazon’s latest chip, known as Trainium.

item.title: Mother and 6 Children Die in Explosion at Their Pennsylvania Home
item.description: The blast on Sunday morning led to a fire that consumed the residence in a rural community northwest of Harrisburg, the authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Bernard Mokam

media:content.media:description: Firefighters at the scene of an explosion and fire on Sunday in Lamar Township, Pa.

item.title: Trump Is Losing His Cheerleaders
item.description: The columnist Michelle Goldberg normally doesn’t see much hope in the Trump era, but on “The Opinions,” she says the cracks in MAGA’s media machine are real — and explains what they could mean for Trump’s future.

item.title: A Rocky, Snow-Barren Ski Season Concludes in Colorado
item.description: Faced with a devastating snow drought and record-high March temperatures, some resorts closed early. But at Aspen Mountain, die-hards celebrated the last day of the season on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra E. Petri and Will Sardinsky

item.title: Opponents Sue to Block Mayor’s Plan to Move Shelter to East Village
item.description: A lawsuit challenges the Mamdani administration’s declaration that closing the 30th Street Shelter was an emergency and was not subject to public notice and other requirements.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth A. Harris

media:content.media:credit: Sara Hylton for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The 30th Street Shelter and Intake Center in Manhattan is slated to close within weeks.

item.title: Student News Quiz: Cease-Fire, Concert Giant, Religion
item.description: Have you been paying attention to current events recently? See how well you can do on this week’s news quiz for students.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Engle

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: México investigará el rol de seguridad de 2 funcionarios de EE. UU. que murieron en un accidente
item.description: La presidenta Claudia Sheinbaum señaló que la investigación se centraría en determinar si la participación de los estadounidenses en una operación violaba las leyes de seguridad de México, no en el accidente.
item.dc:creator: Paulina Villegas

media:content.media:credit: Procuraduría General de Justicia de Chihuahua
media:content.media:description: Un presunto laboratorio de procesamiento de metanfetamina descubierto en el estado de Chihuahua, al norte de México.

item.title: A New Apple C.E.O.
item.description: Also, JD Vance is set to travel to Pakistan for peace talks. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:description: Tim Cook in 2023.

item.title: Scientist Irritated by Lab Colleague Accused in Poisoning Attempt
item.description: The scientist, at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, had built up grievances against his co-worker after five years of working together, court documents said.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman

media:content.media:credit: Lucy Hewett for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The University of Wisconsin campus in Madison.

item.title: George Ariyoshi, 100, America’s First Governor of Asian Descent, Dies
item.description: A Democrat, he served three terms as Hawaii’s governor (1974-86), rising out of a tough district of Honolulu. He tried to diversify an economy heavily dependent on tourism.
item.dc:creator: Robert D. McFadden

media:content.media:description: George R. Ariyoshi speaking in Honolulu in 1977, during his first term as Hawaii’s governor. His election upended a tradition of white and Republican control in the state.

item.title: New Details in the Mass Shooting That Left 8 Dead in Louisiana
item.description: On Monday, city and state officials gave updates on a mass shooting in Shreveport, La., in which a gunman opened fire on his family, killing eight children and injuring two others.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Jill Pickett/The Times-Picayune, via The New Orleans Advocate, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Police tape blocks off a house in Shreveport, La., that is one of the locations tied to the shooting.

item.title: Tiroteo en Teotihuacán: lo que sabemos
item.description: El hombre armado abrió fuego en el yacimiento arqueológico de Teotihuacán, a las afueras de Ciudad de México, antes de quitarse la vida. Entre los heridos había seis estadounidenses.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Cyntia Barrera Díaz

media:content.media:description: Agentes de policía en la Pirámide de la Luna, en las pirámides de Teotihuacán, México, tras un tiroteo mortal el lunes.

item.title: This Parrot Has No Beak, But Is at the Top of the Pecking Order
item.description: Bruce, a disabled kea parrot, is missing his top beak. The bird uses tools to keep himself healthy and developed a jousting technique that has made him the alpha male of his group.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling and Carl Zimmer

item.title: Apple C.E.O.s Through the Years: From Michael Scott (Not That One) to John Ternus
item.description: Apple’s corner office has been a seat from which executives like Steve Jobs changed how we interact with technology.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang and Tripp Mickle

media:content.media:description: The first Apple Macintosh computer in 1984.

item.title: Labor Secretary Steps Down Amid Misconduct Investigation
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
